claim: LangMem SDK enables agents to learn and adapt by optimizing prompts based on user feedback and conversation history
actionable: Use LangMem's prompt optimizer to automatically refine agent instructions from conversation trajectories and feedback
source_title: Build Self-Improving Agents: LangMem Procedural Memory Tutorial

claim: Procedural memory captures rules and instructions inferred from implicit feedback in conversation history, not just explicit corrections
actionable: Train agents to extract implicit procedural knowledge from conversations; let them infer behavioral patterns from user corrections
source_title: Build Self-Improving Agents: LangMem Procedural Memory Tutorial

claim: Multi-prompt optimizer can identify which prompts in a multi-agent system need updating based on outcome-level feedback, not per-agent feedback
actionable: Use multi-prompt optimizer for multi-agent systems to attribute responsibility for outcomes across agents without per-agent supervision
source_title: Build Self-Improving Agents: LangMem Procedural Memory Tutorial

claim: Fixing one bias in LLM evaluation can amplify another; patches don't compose and require holistic process reform, not individual bug fixes
actionable: When fixing evaluation rubric biases, test for side effects; design evals holistically rather than patching individual issues
source_title: Build Self-Improving Agents: LangMem Procedural Memory Tutorial
